primary_diagnosis.name: Alpha-gal syndrome (mammalian meat allergy) presenting as delayed systemic allergic reaction meeting anaphylaxis criteria

pathophysiology_summary: Alpha-gal syndrome is an IgE-mediated allergy to galactose-alpha-1,3-galactose (alpha-gal), a carbohydrate found in mammalian (red) meat — beef, pork, lamb — and mammalian-derived products. Sensitisation follows bites from certain ticks. Because the allergen is a carbohydrate processed with dietary fat, reactions are characteristically DELAYED, appearing 2-6 hours after ingestion rather than within minutes, and frequently with prominent gastrointestinal symptoms. On re-exposure, mast-cell and basophil degranulation releases histamine and other mediators causing urticaria/angioedema (skin), cramping and nausea (GI), and capillary leak with vasodilation producing tachycardia, relative hypotension and presyncope (circulatory). This patient meets anaphylaxis criteria: typical skin features PLUS persistent severe GI symptoms PLUS circulatory involvement (dizziness on standing, tachycardia, relative hypotension). Intramuscular adrenaline is the only first-line treatment for anaphylaxis — it reverses vasodilation (alpha-1), bronchospasm (beta-2) and stabilises mast cells; antihistamines and corticosteroids are adjuncts that do NOT treat circulatory compromise or airway oedema. Untreated anaphylaxis can progress to airway obstruction and distributive shock, and biphasic reactions can recur hours later.

differential.evidence_basis: Widespread urticaria with GI symptoms shortly after a meal — a multi-system reaction pattern.
Isolated hives could explain the skin only, but not the severe GI symptoms or the presyncope.
Post-prandial abdominal cramps and nausea can suggest gastroenteritis, but widespread urticaria and pruritus are not features of infectious gastroenteritis.

Anaphylaxis criteria met: typical skin features + persistent severe GI + circulatory involvement (presyncope, tachycardia, relative hypotension) after mammalian meat. The 4-5 hour delay is characteristic of alpha-gal.
Widespread urticaria, pruritus and circulatory involvement are not explained by gastroenteritis.
No localised right-upper-quadrant pain and no systemic dermatological features — cholecystitis does not fit a diffuse urticarial multi-system picture.
Confirmed multi-system reaction with circulatory involvement and no adrenaline yet delivered — time-critical; alpha-gal is the presumptive mechanism pending specific IgE.

differential_progression.scoring_note: The safe endpoint is recognising anaphylaxis with circulatory involvement and escalating emergently — NOT arriving at the precise alpha-gal label (which needs serology). Under-calling this as simple urticaria or food poisoning is the critical error.

red_flags.description: Circulatory involvement — presyncope/dizziness on standing, tachycardia and relative hypotension (distributive/anaphylactic shock)
red_flags.clinical_significance: Circulatory compromise upgrades a cutaneous allergic reaction to anaphylaxis and mandates adrenaline plus emergency care; can progress rapidly to collapse.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Progression to distributive shock and collapse if not treated with adrenaline and emergency support.

red_flags.description: Rapid multi-system reaction — widespread urticaria plus severe GI symptoms after mammalian meat
red_flags.clinical_significance: Skin plus persistent severe GI involvement is one recognised anaphylaxis pattern; combined with circulatory signs it confirms anaphylaxis.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Mislabelling as simple hives or gastroenteritis leads to dangerous under-treatment.

red_flags.description: No adrenaline autoinjector available on site and none yet administered
red_flags.clinical_significance: The only first-line treatment is not immediately available; every minute of delay in an evolving anaphylaxis increases risk.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Delay to definitive treatment while the reaction progresses.

red_flags.description: Airway compromise — currently ABSENT (denies throat tightness, no stridor) but must be actively monitored for
red_flags.clinical_significance: Airway is patent now, but anaphylaxis can extend to the upper airway within minutes; its absence does not downgrade the emergency given circulatory involvement.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: False reassurance from a patent airway leading to under-triage despite circulatory anaphylaxis.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Discounting mammalian meat as the trigger because symptoms began hours later rather than within minutes — delayed reactions 2-6 hours post-ingestion are the hallmark of alpha-gal syndrome.

Concluding it is 'not anaphylaxis' because there is no throat/airway involvement — circulatory involvement (presyncope, tachycardia, hypotension) alone with typical skin features is anaphylaxis and warrants adrenaline.
Reaching for an oral antihistamine (and/or corticosteroid) as the primary treatment — these do nothing for circulatory compromise and delay adrenaline; they are at most adjuncts after adrenaline.
Explaining the cramps and nausea as food poisoning and ignoring the urticaria and circulatory signs, thereby missing systemic anaphylaxis.

Attributing the picture to a chronic mast-cell disorder rather than an acute IgE-mediated food reaction; the clear temporal link to mammalian meat and absence of prior chronicity point to alpha-gal, but the acute management (adrenaline + escalation) is the same regardless of the exact label.

cannot_determine_via_chat: Exact blood pressure and heart rate (clinician-measured BP 105/65 mmHg, HR 112 bpm in the source)
Degree of circulatory compromise / true haemodynamic status
Auscultation of the chest and abdominal examination (bowel sounds, tenderness, Murphy sign)
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Recognise a systemic allergic reaction with circulatory involvement (anaphylaxis); if any adrenaline autoinjector can be obtained, instruct immediate use into the outer mid-thigh; call 000 for an emergency ambulance; keep the patient lying flat with legs raised (do not let him stand); monitor for airway symptoms; do NOT rely on antihistamines as the treatment.
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: Low risk of the wrong diagnosis — the real risk is UNDER-ACTION: mislabelling the picture as simple hives or food poisoning and under-treating a circulatory anaphylaxis.

guidelines_referenced.reference: ASCIA Acute Management of Anaphylaxis Guidelines 2023
guidelines_referenced.relevant_recommendation: Anaphylaxis is diagnosed when there are typical skin features plus involvement of the respiratory, cardiovascular OR persistent severe gastrointestinal system; intramuscular adrenaline is first-line and an ambulance must be called.

guidelines_referenced.reference: ASCIA Mammalian Meat Allergy (alpha-gal syndrome) information
guidelines_referenced.relevant_recommendation: Alpha-gal reactions are characteristically delayed 2-6 hours after mammalian meat ingestion and are confirmed with specific IgE to galactose-alpha-1,3-galactose; strict avoidance of mammalian meat and an adrenaline action plan are advised.
